page: Microsoft Azure F32-8amds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Famdsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 physical cores operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high resource density of 32.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled, offering 3780 GB of capacity, and the instance is premium storage capable. No GPUs are present on this server configuration.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top 10%. Multi-threaded and multi-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, fall into the average category. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks, along with PassMark memory and database operations, also demonstrate average performance. For language model workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds on small 135M models, but performance drops to the bottom 10% for prompt processing on large 70B models.

Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per core and the inclusion of 3780 GB of local storage, this server offers high memory density relative to its compute footprint. This profile is suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and caching layers like Redis, where the average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance is offset by the massive RAM allocation and strong single-threaded execution.
description: Microsoft Azure F32-8amds_v7 is an x86_64 memory-intensive server from the Famdsv7 family, powered by an 8-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a high memory density of 256.0 GB (32.0 GB per core) and includes 3780 GB of local storage. In benchmark testing, the server exhibits top-tier single-core performance, particularly in stress-ng single-core CPU execution, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations align with average tiers. Small-model LLM text generation is highly efficient, though large-model prompt processing is a notable bottleneck. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and caching workloads that benefit from high RAM capacity and strong single-threaded performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F32-8amds_v7 is an x86_64 memory-intensive server featuring 8 AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for database and memory-heavy workloads.

tagline: An AMD-powered, memory-intensive server featuring high RAM-to-core density and bundled local storage for memory-heavy and single-threaded workloads.

256.0 GB of RAM providing a high 32.0 GB per core density

Top-tier single-core CPU and compression benchmark performance
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and single-threaded tasks
